The surgeon uses a 30 mm curved needle inserted through an 11 mm valveless trocar by Apple. Two needle holders are utilized, one from the central port and another from the lateral port, to manipulate the needle within the surgical field.

The surgeon uses a toothed needle holder and grasper on the right side to securely hold the tissue. After passing the needle through the tissue, the surgeon employs a knot pusher to advance the knot through the trocar.

The surgeon uses a 90-centimeter thread to take two box stitches on the myometrial defect, leaving approximately 40 to 45 centimeters of thread for intracorporeal suturing. Various methods, including simple braided dissolvable sutures, are available for closing the defect.
